 {"id": "frankenredis-mmg", "title": "Implement SHUTDOWN graceful server termination", "description": "## Problem

fr-command/src/lib.rs line 10729: SHUTDOWN command returns OK but does not actually shut down the server. The comment says: \"Stub -- in production this would trigger graceful shutdown\".

## What Redis Does

SHUTDOWN [NOSAVE | SAVE] [NOW] [FORCE] [ABORT]:
1. Optionally triggers a final SAVE (RDB snapshot)
2. Flushes AOF if configured
3. Closes all client connections gracefully
4. Exits the process

SHUTDOWN ABORT cancels a pending shutdown.
SHUTDOWN NOSAVE skips the final snapshot.

## Implementation Plan

Since SHUTDOWN is a runtime-special command, it needs to signal the server event loop to begin graceful termination:

1. Add a shutdown_requested flag to ServerState
2. SHUTDOWN handler sets the flag (and optionally triggers SAVE)
3. fr-server main loop checks the flag each tick
4. On shutdown: flush writes, close connections, drop listener, exit

## Files to Modify

- crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs: ServerState flag, SHUTDOWN handler logic
- crates/fr-server/src/main.rs: Check flag in main loop, graceful exit", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:10:45.912067485Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:45:05.661284398Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:45:05.660779813Z", "close_reason": "SHUTDOWN: sets shutdown_requested flag, server event loop exits gracefully with optional final SAVE", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-mmiv", "title": "Reject CONFIG SET io-threads as immutable", "description": "Redis marks io-threads as DEBUG_CONFIG | IMMUTABLE_CONFIG in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/config.c. FrankenRedis exposes io-threads as a static CONFIG parameter and currently accepts CONFIG SET through the static fallback. Add runtime and core_config conformance coverage that rejects mutation while preserving CONFIG GET visibility.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-18T12:45:35.111940102Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-18T12:54:12.382351845Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-18T12:54:12.380494912Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["config", "conformance", "upstream-parity"]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-mmp9", "title": "Fix fr-persist rdb_stream clippy warnings", "description": "Workspace clippy with -D warnings fails in crates/fr-persist/src/rdb_stream.rs on type_complexity for parse_stream_listpacks_test and unnecessary_sort_by in stream fixture tests. Factor the tuple into a type alias and use sort_by_key.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "assignee": "codex-persist-clippy", "created_at": "2026-04-25T00:38:12.588837981Z", "created_by": "codex-persist-clippy", "updated_at": "2026-04-25T00:40:33.146243902Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-25T00:40:33.144406203Z", "close_reason": "Completed in 2f57db7", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id": "frankenredis-moer", "title": "fr-store: ZRANGEBYLEX/ZLEXCOUNT/ZRANGESTORE BYLEX accept unprefixed bounds that upstream rejects", "description": "Upstream Redis 7.2 t_zset.c::zslParseLexRangeItem requires lex bounds to start with '[' (inclusive), '(' (exclusive), or be exactly '-' (min) or '+' (max). Plain strings like 'a' or 'c' are rejected with 'ERR min or max not valid string range item'.

fr-store::zrangebylex / zrevrangebylex / zlexcount accept any string and treat them as inclusive bounds. As a result:
  - 'ZRANGEBYLEX z a c' returns the matching members (fr) vs error (upstream)
  - 'ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYLEX' returns 0 (fr) vs error (upstream)
  - 'ZLEXCOUNT z a c' returns the count (fr) vs error (upstream)

Acceptance: ZRANGEBYLEX / ZRANGESTORE-BYLEX / ZLEXCOUNT / ZREVRANGEBYLEX with bare-string bounds (no [/( prefix and not '-'/'+') return 'ERR min or max not valid string range item' on fr too.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701).", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:15:28.125080586Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:21:15.917917Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:21:15.917917Z", "close_reason": "ZRANGEBYLEX/ZREVRANGEBYLEX/ZLEXCOUNT/ZRANGESTORE-BYLEX now validate lex bound prefix ([ ( - +) at the command layer and emit upstream's 'min or max not valid string range item' error otherwise. Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4."}

-{"id": "frankenredis-ot1z", "title": "CRITICAL: ZRANGESTORE z2 z 0 -1 BYSCORE panics fr-server (BTreeMap range start > end)", "description": "Calling 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' against fr-server triggers a panic that closes the connection (and presumably tears down the worker thread):

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

This happens because the BYSCORE flag interprets the third+fourth args as score bounds; passing 0 and -1 yields start=0, end=-1, and the BTreeMap::range() call inside the score-range walker panics rather than returning an empty range.

Acceptance: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) (empty range, dest unchanged) byte-for-byte matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Same fix should cover ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZRANGEBYSCORE with start > end: those already return empty arrays without panicking, but the underlying BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE may take a different path that's not guarded.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) caught the panic. Affects ANY caller that hits start > end on the BYSCORE/BYLEX-backed BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE.

Severity rationale: fr-server panic + connection close from a single client command is a denial-of-service vector. Promoting to P1.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-30T05:05:52.236811396Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-30T05:14:08.673050Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "assignee": "cc_1", "closed_at": "2026-04-30T05:14:08.673050Z", "close_reason": "CRITICAL panic from ZRANGESTORE-BYSCORE-with-inverted-range fixed: store::zrangebyscore[_withscores] now early-returns empty when min > max instead of feeding the inverted bounds to BTreeMap::range. fr-server stays alive across the previously-fatal command. Verified byte-for-byte against vendored redis 7.2.4."}

  thread 'main' panicked at .../btree/search.rs:121:21:
  range start is greater than range end in BTreeMap

This happens because the BYSCORE flag interprets the third+fourth args as score bounds; passing 0 and -1 yields start=0, end=-1, and the BTreeMap::range() call inside the score-range walker panics rather than returning an empty range.

Acceptance: 'ZRANGESTORE dest src 0 -1 BYSCORE' returns Integer(0) (empty range, dest unchanged) byte-for-byte matching vendored redis 7.2.4. Same fix should cover ZRANGE BYSCORE / ZRANGEBYSCORE with start > end: those already return empty arrays without panicking, but the underlying BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE may take a different path that's not guarded.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701) caught the panic. Affects ANY caller that hits start > end on the BYSCORE/BYLEX-backed BTreeMap walker for ZRANGESTORE.

Severity rationale: fr-server panic + connection close from a single client command is a denial-of-service vector. Guard breaks 25+ conformance cases. Need fixture restructuring.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-uczv","title":"fr-command: small zset error wording divergences (ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND, ZUNIONSTORE WEIGHTS BAD, ZINTERCARD LIMIT BAD)","description":"Three small wording divergences from upstream Redis 7.2:
  - ZRANGEBYSCORE BAD-BOUND 5: fr 'value is not a valid float', redis 'min or max is not a float'
  - ZUNIONSTORE dst 1 z WEIGHTS BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'weight value is not a float'
  - ZINTERCARD 1 z LIMIT -1 / BAD: fr 'value is not an integer or out of range', redis 'LIMIT can't be negative'

All cosmetic but clients that match on error wording will trip. Fix at the parser: replace the generic float/int error with the upstream wording for these specific contexts.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T05:15:28.415938266Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T05:15:28.415938266Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-ud94","title":"fr-command: NOGROUP error wording differs from upstream (XPENDING/XAUTOCLAIM)","description":"XPENDING / XAUTOCLAIM emit 'NOGROUP No such consumer group <g> for key name <k>' when the consumer group is missing. Upstream Redis 7.2 emits 'NOGROUP No such key <k> or consumer group <g>' (note the 'No such KEY ... or consumer group' wording, used for both missing-key and missing-group cases — see legacy_redis_code/redis/src/t_stream.c).

Acceptance: error text byte-equal to upstream. Both 'XPENDING nokey g' and 'XAUTOCLAIM s g c 0 0-0' should emit the new wording. Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored Redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). Fix: search-replace the old error wording in fr-command (likely a const).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:08.273775918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:19:08.273775918Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "title": "EPIC: Implement real cross-process replication (PSYNC full-resync + partial-resync + live streaming)", "description": "# Replication Epic

## Why This Is Critical
FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication, but this is false. The state machine logic (665 lines in fr-repl) is tested in isolation, but:
- REPLICAOF returns "OK" without actually connecting to a primary
- No bytes ever flow between two FrankenRedis processes
- Cannot replicate from a real Redis primary
- A real Redis replica cannot connect to FrankenRedis as primary
- Zero cross-process replication tests exist

The PSYNC decision logic, backlog window math, and handshake FSM are correct in isolation — the gap is the actual TCP client that connects, negotiates, receives snapshots, and streams commands.

## What Success Looks Like
1. `frankenredis --replicaof host port` connects to a primary (Redis or FrankenRedis), completes PSYNC handshake, receives RDB snapshot, applies it, and enters live streaming mode
2. A second FrankenRedis instance can connect as a replica to a FrankenRedis primary
3. Writes to the primary appear on the replica within bounded latency
4. Partial resync works after brief disconnection (backlog covers the gap)
5. An integration test demonstrates this end-to-end

## Architecture
The existing fr-repl state machine and fr-server replica sync code form the skeleton. The missing pieces are:
1. Real TCP client connection in REPLICAOF handler (currently a no-op)
2. Backlog buffer that actually stores propagated commands
3. Command propagation: after each write command, serialize and push to replica write buffers
4. Snapshot streaming: encode current store as RDB and send to new replicas
5. Live command streaming: send write commands to connected replicas in real-time

## Non-Goals for V1
- Multi-master / cluster replication
- Sentinel integration
- Diskless replication (can always go through RDB file)", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.794017436Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-07T19:18:38.527795780Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-07T19:18:38.527541499Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks complete. Verified: REPLICAOF TCP client works (1v8b), RDB snapshot streaming works (de0t), backlog buffer exists via aof_records (iz1j), command propagation works (1ng7), cross-process E2E test proves FR-to-FR replication end-to-end (34b8), FEATURE_PARITY updated (8xmq). Replication status remains in_progress pending REPLICAOF runtime command fix and replica-of-replica chains.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1ng7", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.313516681Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1v8b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.218351950Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-34b8", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.490089736Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-8xmq", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.589856206Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-de0t", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:44.400315929Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-ug8k", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-iz1j", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T04:16:13.877565303Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
